title: Why is the rescue taking so long?

meanings.usage: 常用被动语态 be trapped in
可指物理陷阱或比喻意义上的困境
常用 rescue sb from sth
可作主语或宾语
bring to the surface 带到地面
不及物动词
rescue operations 救援行动
数学用语
常用复数形式 explosives
作定语修饰名词
常用复数 vibrations
非正式用语，意为'直觉感受'
不及物动词，不能带宾语
可用作主语或宾语
drill a hole 钻一个洞
作名词时指工具或训练
特指救援用的特殊吊舱
航天或潜水用语
比 under 更正式，多用于书面语
enable sb to do sth 使某人能够做某事
描述人的情绪状态
描述事物或环境
confident that + 从句 表示'确信...'
confident of/about doing sth
progress smoothly 进展顺利
描述动作或过程
lose heart 是固定搭配，heart 不加 s
与 lose heart 相反的是 take heart 或 pluck up courage

examples.en: If they are not brought to the surface soon, they may lose their lives.
examples.zh: 如果不尽快把他们救到地面，他们可能会失去生命。

phrases.usage: 用完，耗尽（主动含义，主语消耗完某物）

examples.en: Though they are running out of food and drink, the men are cheerful.
examples.zh: 虽然他们的食物和饮料快用完了，但这些人仍然很乐观。

examples.en: The microphone has enabled the men to keep in touch with their closest relatives.
examples.zh: 麦克风使这些人能够与他们的至亲保持联系。

examples.en: If they knew how difficult it was to drill through the hard rock, they would lose heart.
examples.zh: 如果他们知道在硬岩石上钻孔有多难，他们会失去信心的。

phrases.usage: 证明是困难的，结果很困难

examples.en: However, rescue operations are proving difficult.
examples.zh: 然而，救援行动证明是困难的。

examples.en: They would have completed the job in a few hours.
examples.zh: 他们本可以在几小时内完成这项工作的。

phrases.usage: 事实上，实际上（表示与预期相反的情况）

examples.en: As it is, they have been drilling for 16 hours and they still have a long way to go.
examples.zh: 事实上，他们已经钻了16个小时了，但仍有很长的路要走。

phrases.phrase: have a long way to go
phrases.usage: 还有很长的路要走，还有很多工作要做

examples.en: They still have a long way to go.
examples.zh: 他们仍然有很长的路要走。

examples.en: The men are confident that they will get out soon.
examples.zh: 这些人确信他们很快就能出去。

examples.en: They have been told that rescue operations are progressing smoothly.
examples.zh: 他们被告知救援行动正在顺利进行。

grammar.title: 第三条件句（Third Conditional）
grammar.definition: 表示与过去事实完全相反的假设，结构为'如果过去做了某事（但实际没做），结果会不同'
grammar.structure: If + 主语 + had + 过去分词，主语 + would have + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于讨论与过去事实相反的假设情况，表达遗憾或想象的结果

examples.en: If there had not been a hard layer of rock beneath the soil, they would have completed the job in a few hours.
examples.zh: 如果土壤下面没有那层硬岩石，他们本可以在几个小时内完成这项工作的。

examples.en: If they had known the truth, they would have prepared better.
examples.zh: 如果他们早知道真相，他们就会准备得更好。

grammar.title: 现在完成进行时（Present Perfect Continuous）
grammar.definition: 表示从过去开始一直持续到现在的动作，可能还在继续，并强调动作的持续性
grammar.structure: 主语 + have/has + been + 动词-ing
grammar.usage: 强调动作的持续性，通常与 for、since、all day 等时间状语连用

examples.en: They have been drilling for 16 hours.
examples.zh: 他们已经钻了16个小时了。

examples.en: Six men have been trapped in the mine for seventeen hours.
examples.zh: 六个人已经在矿井里被困了17个小时。

grammar.title: 被动语态（Passive Voice）在不同时间中的应用
grammar.definition: 当主语是动作的承受者时，使用被动语态。不同时态的被动形式体现了动作发生的时间
grammar.structure: be + 过去分词（不同时态的 be 形式）
grammar.usage: 本课展示了多种被动语态：现在进行时被动（are being brought）、现在完成时被动（has been lowered）、一般过去时被动（was lowered）

examples.en: If they are not brought to the surface soon... (现在时被动)
examples.zh: 如果他们不尽快被带到地面...

examples.en: A microphone, which was lowered into the mine two hours ago... (过去时被动)
examples.zh: 两个小时前被放到矿井里的麦克风...

examples.en: They intend to bring the men up in a special capsule. (主动语态)
examples.zh: 他们打算用特殊的吊舱把这些人救上来。

sentencePatterns.pattern: If + there had not been + 名词，主语 + would have + 过去分词

original.en: If there had not been a hard layer of rock beneath the soil, they would have completed the job in a few hours.
original.zh: 如果土壤下面没有那层硬岩石，他们本可以在几个小时内完成这项工作的。

imitations.en: If there had not been the heavy rain, we would have arrived on time.
imitations.zh: 如果没有那场大雨，我们本可以准时到达的。

imitations.en: If there had not been your help, I would have failed the exam.
imitations.zh: 如果没有你的帮助，我考试就会不及格了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + have/has been + 动词-ing + for + 时间段

original.en: They have been drilling for 16 hours.
original.zh: 他们已经钻了16个小时了。

imitations.en: I have been studying English for two hours.
imitations.zh: 我已经学了两个小时的英语了。

imitations.en: She has been waiting for you since morning.
imitations.zh: 她从早上起就一直在等你。

sentencePatterns.pattern: If + 主语 + are/is not + 过去分词，主语 + may + 动词原形

original.en: If they are not brought to the surface soon they may lose their lives.
original.zh: 如果不尽快把他们救到地面，他们可能会失去生命。

imitations.en: If the medicine is not taken in time, the patient may die.
imitations.zh: 如果不及时服药，病人可能会死。

imitations.en: If the problem is not solved today, we may face greater difficulties.
imitations.zh: 如果今天不解决这个问题，我们可能会面临更大的困难。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + be + 形容词 + that + 从句

original.en: The men are cheerful and confident that they will get out soon.
original.zh: 这些人很乐观，并确信他们很快就能出去。

imitations.en: I am sure that he will succeed.
She is worried that she might miss the train.
imitations.zh: 她担心她可能会错过火车。
